reasoning: The kernel performs 1 FLOP (addition) per element while reading 4 bytes (one float from `in`) and writing 4 bytes (one float to `out`), yielding an arithmetic intensity of ~1/8 = 0.125 FLOP/byte, which is far below the A10G ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte. With n=2^26 elements, this moves ~512 MB of data with minimal computation, meaning the kernel will saturate DRAM bandwidth well before compute units are fully utilized. DRAM utilization should be high as the access pattern is fully coalesced and the working set is large.
